selection_process: Candidates preparing for this recruitment should understand that selection happens in multiple stages
starting with document scrutiny and ending with a merit list based purely on written exam performance. Each stage acts as a filter before the next
so clearing one stage is necessary to proceed to the following one.

Scrutiny of Applications - All applications are checked against the eligibility criteria and required documents; admit cards are issued only to shortlisted candidates. Document Verification - Shortlisted candidates must bring original documents plus two self-attested photocopy sets
as instructed in their admit card
before appearing for the written exam. Biometric Verification - Thumb impression and photograph are captured during initial document verification and re-verified at every subsequent stage
including at the time of joining. Written Examination - A pen-paper based
bilingual exam with 80 objective-type questions covering General English
and relevant trade questions. Each correct answer carries one mark
and there is no negative marking. Skill/Trade Test - Only candidates who qualify the written exam are called for this test

which is qualifying in nature (not counted for merit). Merit List - Prepared strictly based on marks obtained in the written examination and published on the official Indian Coast Guard website along with further instructions.
how_to_apply: Applicants asking how to submit their form correctly should follow these steps precisely, since even minor errors like missing photographs or overwriting can lead to rejection.
Fill the application form in English or Hindi, strictly as per the format given in Annexure-I.
Affix a self-attested colour photograph on the form.
Attach self-attested photocopies of all required documents (originals are not to be sent).
Enclose the blank self-addressed envelope with Rs. 50/- postal stamp.
Superscribe the envelope in BOLD letters with "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF ENGINE DRIVER" and the category applied for, i.e., EWS.
Send the complete application by ordinary post only to: The Commander, Coast Guard Region (A&N), Post Box No. 716, Haddo (PO), Sri Vijaya Puram - 744102, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
Ensure the application reaches within 45 days from the date of publication in Employment News, and no later than 11 August 2026.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver Recruitment

rows.event: Last Date for Receipt of Applications

rows.date: Within 45 days from date of publication in Employment News

content_sections.heading: Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: Candidates often want to know exactly how many posts are available and under which category before applying. For this recruitment cycle, the Indian Coast Guard has notified only one post, reserved specifically for the EWS category. It is worth noting that this vacancy count is subject to variation, meaning the Coast Guard may revise the number of posts before the selection process concludes.

content_sections.heading: Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver Recruitment 2026 - Qualification and Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering whether they qualify for the Engine Driver post can check both the educational requirement and the age bracket below. Meeting both conditions is mandatory, and the desirable qualification can add extra weight to an application even though it is not compulsory. Essential: Matriculation pass (or its equivalent) from a recognized Board, along with a Certificate of Competency as Engine Driver from a recognized Government Institute or equivalent. Desirable: Two years of service as Sarang on a vessel of over four hundred Boat Horse Power. The age limit for the Engine Driver post is between 18 and 30 years, calculated as on the last date of receipt of applications, i.e., 11 August 2026. Age relaxation is available up to 40 years for Government servants, as well as for Ex-Servicemen, in accordance with orders issued by the Central Government.

content_sections.heading: Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates preparing for this recruitment should understand that selection happens in multiple stages, starting with document scrutiny and ending with a merit list based purely on written exam performance. Each stage acts as a filter before the next, so clearing one stage is necessary to proceed to the following one. Scrutiny of Applications - All applications are checked against the eligibility criteria and required documents; admit cards are issued only to shortlisted candidates. Document Verification - Shortlisted candidates must bring original documents plus two self-attested photocopy sets, as instructed in their admit card, before appearing for the written exam. Biometric Verification - Thumb impression and photograph are captured during initial document verification and re-verified at every subsequent stage, including at the time of joining. Written Examination - A pen-paper based, one-hour, bilingual exam with 80 objective-type questions covering General English, General Knowledge, Simple Maths, and relevant trade questions. Each correct answer carries one mark, and there is no negative marking. Skill/Trade Test - Only candidates who qualify the written exam are called for this test, which is qualifying in nature (not counted for merit). Merit List - Prepared strictly based on marks obtained in the written examination and published on the official Indian Coast Guard website along with further instructions.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver Post
content_sections.text: Applicants asking how to submit their form correctly should follow these steps precisely, since even minor errors like missing photographs or overwriting can lead to rejection. Fill the application form in English or Hindi, strictly as per the format given in Annexure-I. Affix a self-attested colour photograph on the form. Attach self-attested photocopies of all required documents (originals are not to be sent). Enclose the blank self-addressed envelope with Rs. 50/- postal stamp. Superscribe the envelope in BOLD letters with "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF ENGINE DRIVER" and the category applied for, i.e., EWS. Send the complete application by ordinary post only to: The Commander, Coast Guard Region (A&N), Post Box No. 716, Haddo (PO), Sri Vijaya Puram - 744102, Andaman & Nicobar Islands. Ensure the application reaches within 45 days from the date of publication in Employment News, and no later than 11 August 2026.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required with the Application
content_sections.text: Before sealing the envelope, candidates should double check that all the following self-attested photocopies (with name and date) are enclosed, since incomplete documentation is one of the most common reasons for rejection: Valid Photo ID proof Date of birth proof (Class X Passing Certificate OR Birth Certificate) Matriculation or equivalent marksheet and certificate Aadhar Card Latest EWS Category certificate as per the format in Annexure-II Certificate of Competency as Engine Driver from a recognized Government institute or equivalent No Objection Certificate (NOC) from employer, if presently serving in any Government organization Two latest passport-size colour photographs Note: All documents must be dated on or before 11 August 2026, and this same date is also the crucial date for determining age, educational qualification, and EWS category eligibility.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Indian Coast Guard Engine Driver post?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of applications is 11 August 2026, and applications must reach within 45 days from the date of publication in Employment News

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the Engine Driver post?
faq.answer: There is 1 vacancy, reserved for the Economically Weaker Section (EWS) category, and this number is subject to variation.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for this post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Matriculation pass (or equivalent) from a recognized Board, along with a Certificate of Competency as Engine Driver from a recognized Government Institute or equivalent.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Engine Driver recruitment?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 30 years, relaxable up to 40 years for Government servants and Ex-Servicemen as per Central Government orders.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for this post?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the notification. However, candidates must enclose a blank envelope with a Rs. 50/- postal stamp, addressed to themselves.

faq.question: Q6. Can the application be submitted online?
faq.answer: No, the application must be submitted offline, by ordinary post only, to the Commander, Coast Guard Region (A&N) at the address specified in the notification

faq.question: Q7. What does the selection process for Engine Driver include?
faq.answer: Selection involves scrutiny of applications, document verification, biometric verification, a written examination, a qualifying skill/trade test, and a final merit list based on written exam marks.

faq.question: Q8. What is the pattern of the written examination?
faq.answer: It is a pen-paper based, one-hour, bilingual exam with 80 objective-type questions covering General English, General Knowledge, Simple Maths, and trade-related topics, with one mark per correct answer and no negative marking.
